properties.titulo_evento: China missile test draws criticism from Australia, New Zealand, Japan
properties.descricao: Countries raise concerns after Chinese military test-launches ballistic missile from submarine in the Pacific Ocean.

properties.titulo_evento: Russian attacks on Ukraine kill 11 on eve of NATO summit, authorities say
properties.descricao: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had warned of a likely attack ahead of meeting with US President Donald Trump.

properties.titulo_evento: Haaland inspired Norway stun Brazil in historic World Cup knockout win
properties.descricao: Norway fans celebrate with viking rows from outside the stadium in New Jersey to Oslo as the team stuns Brazil.

properties.titulo_evento: OPEC+ countries say they will expand monthly oil production
properties.descricao: Seven OPEC+ members, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, to increase output by 188,000 barrels per day.

properties.titulo_evento: Sports stadium becomes home for victims of the Venezuela earthquakes
properties.descricao: A sports stadium in La Guaira state has been turned into a makeshift home and logistics centre for thousands of victims.

properties.titulo_evento: Neymar quits international football after Brazil’s World Cup knockout loss
properties.descricao: The injury-plagued striker converted a penalty but failed to take his team into the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup.

properties.titulo_evento: Belgium ‘astonished’ at FIFA’s U-turn on Balogun red card for USA match
properties.descricao: Belgium's football federation is 'investigating all potential options' to safeguard football after FIFA's U-turn.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: Khamenei’s funeral procession under way in Tehran
properties.descricao: The funeral procession is expected to last 10-12 hours and has drawn crowds of hundreds of thousands of mourners.

properties.titulo_evento: How a son rescued his father from the rubble of Venezuela’s earthquakes
properties.descricao: After the June 24 earthquakes, ex-firefighter Jesus Garcia found himself working to save his dad and younger brothers.

properties.titulo_evento: Haaland scores twice as Norway stun Brazil 2-1 in World Cup 2026 last 16
properties.descricao: Haaland's brace takes him to seven goals for the tournament as Norway book a quarterfinal against Mexico or England.

properties.titulo_evento: Palestinian baby dies in West Bank after Israel blocks urgent medical care
properties.descricao: Israeli forces also shoot dead a 16-year-old boy in the occupied West Bank and kill two Palestinians in Gaza.

properties.titulo_evento: At least 11 killed in second Russian strikes on Kyiv in a week
properties.descricao: Rescue efforts are continuing at more than 20 locations, the city's top military administrator says.

properties.titulo_evento: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
properties.descricao: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.

properties.titulo_evento: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
properties.descricao: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

properties.titulo_evento: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
properties.descricao: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.

properties.titulo_evento: Girl trapped in Venezuela quake 'survived on ketchup and cheese'
properties.descricao: BBC international correspondent Yogita Limayee meets 12-year-old Fabiana, who was trapped in Venezuela's devastating earthquakes.

properties.titulo_evento: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
properties.descricao: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.

properties.titulo_evento: China conducts long-range ballistic missile test in South Pacific
properties.descricao: China said it test-launched a long-range ballistic missile in the South Pacific on Monday, after countries in the region reported having been warned of an imminent test.

properties.titulo_evento: Live: Russian missile attack on Kyiv region kills several
properties.descricao: Russia struck Ukraine's Kyiv region with ballistic missiles on Monday, killing at least 11 people and wounding dozens, authorities said, on the eve of a NATO summit in Turkey. The assault was the second on the capital and its surroundings in less th...

properties.titulo_evento: Ten-man England survive Mexico comeback to knock out World Cup co-hosts
properties.descricao: Ten-man England clinched a famous 3-2 win at the Azteca Stadium on Sunday as they held off a spirited Mexico fightback to set up a World Cup quarter-final against Norway. Jude Bellingham scored twice and Harry Kane netted from the penalty spot to ha...

properties.titulo_evento: FIFA lifts Balogun's World Cup suspension after Trump calls Infantino
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump called FIFA president Gianni Infantino and asked him to review the World Cup suspension of US striker Folarin Balogun, a source familiar with the matter said on Sunday. FIFA later lifted Balogun's one-match ban, clearing hi...

properties.titulo_evento: Zelensky warns of new Russian attacks as battle for Kostyantynivka continues
properties.descricao: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Sunday that fighting was continuing for the eastern town of Kostyantynivka, rejecting Moscow's claim that Russian forces had captured the strategic settlement. Zelensky also warned Russia was preparing ...

properties.titulo_evento: Some Lebanese Christian villages 'asked to be annexed to Israel', Netanyahu says
properties.descricao: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Sunday that some Christian villages in southern Lebanon had asked to be annexed by Israel for protection from Hezbollah, a claim made as he reaffirmed that Israeli troops would remain in southern Leb...

properties.titulo_evento: Thieves steal around €4 million in jewellery from French museum
properties.descricao: Jewellery worth several million euros has been stolen from the Lalique Museum in northeastern France after burglars broke into the site early on Sunday and targeted its jewellery collection. Around 20 pieces were taken in the raid, with losses estim...

properties.titulo_evento: Why Iran's unseen leader remains in the shadows
properties.descricao: The images emerging from Tehran are striking. Tens of thousands of mourners dressed in black have gathered at the Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla for the funeral and lying in state of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, following his assassination on the opening da...

properties.titulo_evento: Isaac Del Toro overcomes mechanical setback to win Tour de France 2nd stage
properties.descricao: Isaac del ​Toro persevered, despite a mechanical issue and a prolonged ​wait ‌to ⁠change his bike, to ‌mount a late challenge ⁠and win the second stage of ​the Tour ‌de France in a time trial in Barcelona on Sunday, becoming the ‌second Mexican ride...

properties.titulo_evento: Marine Le Pen faces key appeal ruling over 2027 presidential bid
properties.descricao: France's far-right chief Marine Le Pen faces a key ruling next week that will determine if she can run in the 2027 presidential elections, in what is seen as her best chance at the top job. A lower court handed the 57-year-old politician a five-year...

properties.titulo_evento: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei absent from mass funeral for father
properties.descricao: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s son and successor was not among the family members and thousands of mourners who turned out Sunday for a second day of ceremonies to honour Iran’s late supreme leader. Mojtaba Khamenei has remained out of the public eye sinc...

properties.titulo_evento: Tour de France stage to go ahead with changes as fires ravage southern Europe
properties.descricao: An "adapted" format of the third stage of the Tour de France will go ahead on Monday after wildfires sweeping southern France put the cycling stage in jeopardy, a top official said Sunday. The blaze, which has already scorched 1,500 hectares near th...

properties.titulo_evento: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific
properties.descricao: Beijing carried out the launch in what is known as a nuclear-free zone due to a longstanding treaty against testing nuclear missiles in the area.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Federal Cabinet to approve draft 2027 budget
properties.descricao: The budget draft provides for more borrowing than initially planned, as well as a big increase in defense spending. Meanwhile, three men are facing trial over a massive stash of illegal weapons. Follow DW for more.

properties.titulo_evento: Australia, Fiji sign defense pact to counter China in Pacific
properties.descricao: Fiji, which had grown closer to Beijing under its former leadership, will now consult Canberra on security developments.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine: Several killed in renewed attacks on Kyiv
properties.descricao: At least nine people were killed when Russian ballistic missiles hit the Ukrainian capital. This follows the large-scale attack on Kyiv last Thursday.

properties.titulo_evento: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
properties.descricao: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

properties.titulo_evento: Students leave Gaza to continue studies, others in limbo
properties.descricao: More than three school years since the start of Israel's war in Gaza, more Palestinian students are restarting studies at universities abroad. Education has yet to return to normal, as schools expand online teaching.

properties.titulo_evento: Cargo ship reportedly attacked in Red Sea off Yemen
properties.descricao: The UK Maritime Trade Office said a vessel was attacked 30 nautical miles southwest of Hodeida, which is under control of the Houthi rebels. The Iran-backed group recently vowed to resume attacks in the Red Sea.

properties.titulo_evento: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
properties.descricao: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
properties.descricao: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelenskyy demands Western support after Russian strikes kill 11 in Kyiv
properties.descricao: "The United States and Europe have enough strength to stop this terror," the Ukrainian leader wrote on X.

properties.titulo_evento: France’s far-right star Bardella is about to find out whether he’s running for president
properties.descricao: The 30-year-old National Rally president will become his party's candidate if Marine Le Pen's appeal fails on Tuesday.

properties.titulo_evento: ‘Shame on you’: Belgium accuses FIFA of caving to Trump ahead of World Cup clash
properties.descricao: Politicians across the political spectrum blasted the decision to clear American star Folarin Balogun to play Monday.

properties.titulo_evento: OPEC Plus Pledges to Pump More Even as Oil Prices Fall
properties.descricao: The cartel’s decision comes as U.S.-Iran talks to end the war have eased pressure on oil supplies, even as broader market and political volatility persists.
